lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: That's the lot of them. Let's head back up.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: That was too close. You nearly got us both killed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand... What are those things? Are they zombies?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: You really want me to believe that you don't know anything about this place?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I already told you, I wasn't lying.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Well I don't have any reason to trust that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: With how dangerous this place is, do you really have a choice?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Alright, fine. But I have no desire to see you get us killed, so you'd better wisen up fast.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm listening.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: It all starts with the Old Ones. They lived here long before humans came to these lands, and these ruins here were once their home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And these Old Ones discovered soul transference?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: They did, and they did so long before House Arceuus even existed. It was their final attempt to overcome their extinction, but they failed.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: What you see around you is the result of their failure. It's known as the Strangler.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Strangler?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: It's some sort of parasite. It was presumably meant to play a role in their transference rituals, but instead it destroyed them. It has remained here ever since.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hence the Strangled?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Exactly. Plenty of unwary fools have fallen victim to the Strangler. If you don't take care, you will share their fate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how do we deal with them?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: The trick is to maintain your distance. They're relatively passive unless you get too close, but if you disturb one, the rest will quickly come running.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: If you need to fight one, try to do it away from the rest!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: And stay off of the tendrils! You'll alert the lot of them to your location! It's like a network.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Keep my distance and stay off the tendrils. I can do that. So what's our next move?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: The fact that you're still alive tells me you broke into my home and stole some of the potion I made.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'Broke in' is maybe a bit strong...

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Call it what you want, it's not important now. What is important is that I believe I can make a more powerful potion using ingredients found here.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: If all goes well, this potion will provide increased immunity against the Strangler. If either of us are to find what we want here, it will be essential.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what ingredients do we need?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: As you've no doubt seen, the Strangler infects plant life as well. However, I've come across a small number of plants that seem to have developed immunity.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: First, there's a herb growing atop the cliff just north west of the temple. Second, there are some berries in an old camp on the coast to the south west.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I can gather those.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I need to gather some additional ingredients from within the temple. I will meet you back here once I'm done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Kasonde! Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Damn it! Damn it! This can't happen! It can't!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Kasonde?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Kasonde...

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: It's fine! This doesn't change anything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But the infection...

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: The infection means nothing!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I was a dead man either way...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Koreps Syndrome. Common among botanists apparently. You'd have thought we'd have put some effort into finding a cure, but I guess no one ever thinks they'll be the unlucky one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Kasonde, I...

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Don't. Whatever you have to say, I don't want to hear it. Do you know how many times I've heard the word 'sorry'? Do you know how many times people have told me they understand?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Well they don't. I spent every day working away, telling myself that one day I'd finally be able to live my life, but it was all a lie. That was my life, and it was all so... meaningless!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I tried everything. I went to every healer, ever mage, every priest.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I went to House Arceuus, and they turned me away! What gives them the right?!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: So no, this doesn't change anything. The Old Ones are my last hope. I either find their secrets, or...

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Look, we're wasting time here! There's a ritual site to the west. Presumably it's where the Strangler was unleashed. It's my best hope now, and it may well be where you'll find what you're after as well.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Take this potion and add those ingredients you gathered to it. It should strengthen your immunity against the Strangler. Now go!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Well? Who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm player, and you are?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I think you already know the answer to that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... Do I?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Shaw. Kasonde Shaw. Don't go acting like you haven't been following me for some time now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, of course. Those old ruins.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Exactly. Perhaps you took me for a fool, but I was on to you from the start. Considering what we learnt, I knew it was only a matter of time before you showed up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean soul transference? Not actually something that interests me, to be honest.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Don't take me for a fool! If you had no interest in the Old Ones' secrets, why would you have followed me to those ruins? And why would you have come here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To be honest, I didn't even know what an Old One was. I still don't really. I was just exploring when I found your camp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As for why I'm here, I'm looking for information about someone called Vardorvis.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Really? You want me to believe that all of this is a coincidence?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... yes.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: You're lying! You're lying and...

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Hang on... do you hear that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hear what?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: The Strangled! Did you disturb any tendrils on your way in here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I may have...?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: They'll be coming. We've got no chance up here. Head down below! Now!
